---
title: "LF Basic Data Stats"
author: "Cheyenne Morgan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


# Setting up 
First setting the working directory
```{r}
getwd()
```

Then we are going to mesh a bunch of code together so not to have the entire table show up here. 
```{r}
also = read.csv("LF_Master_02272026.csv")
head(also)
```
```{r}
names(also)
```

# Outliers in the Data
Now we are going to make an object, I, to represent Soil moisture content (SMC). Then we will transform the SMC variable to z. 
```{r}
names(also)
I=also$SMC
z=((I-mean(I))/sd(I))
```
```{r}
class (z)
```

The mean of z should equal 0
```{r}
mean(z)
```

Pretty good. Now we need to verify that the Std.Dev (2/z) =1
```{r}
sd(z)
```
Also Good!

Now Using z, we will find the values of SMC that are possible outliers
```{r}
z[abs(z)>=2 & abs(z)<=3]
I[abs(z)>=2 & abs(z)<=3]
```

Now we will find the values of SMC that are actual outliers
```{r}
z[abs(z)>3]
I[abs(z)>3]
```
